Sourcing Recommendations: Pre-1995 models preferred (no ECU lock). Parts: All States Ag Parts / Yesterday's Tractors / Shoup. Manuals: Archive.org John Deere collection; Farm Manuals Fast. Training: Hudson Valley Farm Hub Farm Mechanic Basics. Target models: JD 2000/3000 series; Ford 3000-5000; IH 300-504; MF 135-165.

PTO-driven preferred for tractored systems. Walk-behind (BCS 732/853) for homestead scale — excellent repairability; no ECU. Parts: Shoup Manufacturing; brand-specific dealers. Manuals: manufacturer sites + Archive.org. Italian-brand (BCS/Grillo) parts support is strong in North America.

Mechanically simple; no electronics. Wear parts (discs, points, shanks) dominate repair need. Parts: Shoup Manufacturing (30-60% below OEM on wear parts). Manuals: NRCS Tillage Equipment Pocket ID Guide (free); manufacturer operator manuals. Welding skill unlocks DIY frame repair.

Calibration is the critical skill (Tier 1 trainable). Great Plains; JD 750 drill; Kinze — parts through Shoup. Hand seeders (Earthway) at homestead scale: $80-160; full parts catalog available. FarmHack bicycle seeder designs for no-power alternatives. Documentation: manufacturer operator manuals widely available.

Highest open-source coverage. Appropedia agricultural tools wiki; FarmHack culticycle and related designs; OSE GVCS hand tool documentation. Repair = sharpen / replace handle / replace fasteners. Parts: any hardware store. Documentation: iFixit guides; YouTube; wikiHow. Zero electronics. Lowest skill floor of any category.

ATTRA 'Maintaining Irrigation Pumps Motors and Engines' is the definitive free guide. Parts: Grainger; McMaster-Carr for seals/impellers/bearings. Electrical supply houses for motor brushes and capacitors. Zone 5 consideration: freeze protection critical; drain lines before winter. Carburetor rebuild (gasoline pumps) is Tier 1-2 trainable.

Carburetor kit ($15-40) covers majority of failures. Briggs & Stratton; Honda documentation freely available from manufacturers. Annual service prevents 80% of failures. Parts: any outdoor power equipment dealer. Zone 5: store with full fuel treated with stabilizer OR drain before winter. Critical for food preservation and shop power during grid outages.

Knotter system (square baler) is the most complex in-season repair; highest skill demand. New Holland 273/316; JD 336/346 best supported. Agproud Mechanics Corner knotter series is best free technical resource. Parts: Yesterday's Tractors; Abilene Machine. Round baler: simpler mechanism; net wrap system periodic replacement. Training: hands-on only — must practice on actual baler before season.

Specialty equipment; at community scale a pull-behind plot harvester (Almaco; Wintersteiger) or small combine (Gleaner L2; older IH 95) is more practical than modern locked electronics. Parts availability falls sharply for models post-2010. Manuals: Archive.org for older models. High repair complexity; prioritize preventive maintenance over reactive repair. Zone 5: October harvest pressure makes downtime highest-consequence of any equipment type.

Highest open-source coverage after hand tools. FarmHack John Howe Winnower-Thresher: build-it-yourself under $200; complete instructions at farmhack.org. OSU Extension video documentation. Permies.com community knowledge. Commercial options: Almaco; Vogel & Noot for research/small farm scale. Mechanical simplicity: no electronics; belt-driven; straightforward repair.

Not standalone equipment — component of tractor and implements. Frequency is highest of any system. Seal replacement (Tier 1-2 trainable; seal kits $15-80). Pressure testing requires gauge set ($150-400). Documentation: tractor service manual hydraulic section — widely available. Parts: Shoup; any hydraulic supply house. Community must pre-stock seal kits and hydraulic filter for every tractor model in use.

Highly variable by specific equipment. Sprayer: pump seals and nozzle replacement are Tier 1; calibration is Tier 2. Post-pounder: minimal maintenance; weld cracked frame as needed. Skid steer attachments: hydraulic quick-connect service; wear parts. Documentation: manufacturer operator manuals; declining archive availability for specialty equipment. Sourcing: specialty dealers; manufacturer direct.
